title: Problem installing podman from source: sudo make install fails
body:
### Issue Description

I was following the instructions [here](https://podman.io/docs/installation#building-from-source), as per the podman contributors guide. It causes a bit of unnecessary friction for newcomers that would be worth fixing I think. A similar issue was encountered previously: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19755 . In my case, the build worked, but installing with this command failed: 
`sudo make install PREFIX=/usr`

I'm willing to submit a PR for this issue but was unsure of whether editing the docs or the Makefile would be preferred.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `sudo go env GOOS` without having /usr/local/go/bin added to secure_path

### Describe the results you received

The issue in the Makefile is with these two lines: 
```
GO ?= go                                                                        
NATIVE_GOOS := $(shell env -u GOOS $(GO) env GOOS)
```
so this error is encountered:
Required variable NATIVE_GOOS value is undefined, whitespace, or empty.  Stop.`

### Describe the results you expected

A successful installation from source after following the instructions [here](https://podman.io/docs/installation#building-from-source) and running `sudo make install PREFIX=/usr`. 

### podman info output

```yaml


OS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.4.0-dev
API Version:  5.4.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.23.4
Git Commit:   d3cd5098f0abb54279ce4abe594d309e2bc027b0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

virt-manager

comments.body:
Can confirm make install fails.

```
sudo make install PREFIX=/usr
...
...
mkdir -p docs/build/man
( echo "% podman-rootless 7"; echo; sed -e '/logo.*\.png/d' <rootless.md ) >docs/source/markdown/podman-rootless.7.md.tmp.$ && \
	mv docs/source/markdown/podman-rootless.7.md.tmp.$ docs/source/markdown/podman-rootless.7.md
( echo "% podman-troubleshooting 7"; echo; sed -e '/logo.*\.png/d' <troubleshooting.md ) >docs/source/markdown/podman-troubleshooting.7.md.tmp.$ && \
	mv docs/source/markdown/podman-troubleshooting.7.md.tmp.$ docs/source/markdown/podman-troubleshooting.7.md
/usr/bin/bash: line 1: go: command not found
/usr/bin/bash: line 1: go: command not found
env: ‘go’: No such file or directory
env: ‘go’: No such file or directory
Makefile:191: *** Required variable NATIVE_GOOS value is undefined, whitespace, or empty.  Stop.

```
Looks like you don't have golang installed?

> Looks like you don't have golang installed?

Hi Daniel, 

I have v1.23.4 installed:

```
OS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.4.0-dev
API Version:  5.4.0-dev
**Go Version:   go1.23.4**
Git Commit:   d3cd5098f0abb54279ce4abe594d309e2bc027b0
```

This issue doesn't prevent me from building podman - `sudo make install` fails, though. I followed the instructions on go.dev for linux when installing go (https://go.dev/doc/install).

Running `sudo go env GOOS` fails with `sudo: go: command not found`. My workaround was just to replace this line in the Makefile: 
`GO ?= go`
with this:
`GO ?=/usr/local/go/bin/go`

Or perhaps the docs could be changed to include something like this command: 
```
sudo env PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin make install PREFIX=/usr
```
Please open a PR to change the docs.
